summary: initial baseline — but KG file was missing in worktree (kg_expansion code path skipped entirely) so the number reflected a degenerate pipeline. Superseded by iter 001b.

baseline recaptured after (a) copying .knowledge_graph.json from main repo so GraphQueryExpander loads, (b) replacing kg-14 query with one that matches real KG entities, (c) fixing regression guard to assert top-K only on kg-kind queries (cold queries latency-only). Target: p95 ≤ 2425ms (20% reduction). Per-stage: rerank p50=1448/p95=1944ms dominates; embedding cold spikes to p95=1642ms; kg_expansion 0.5-1.4ms (dead surface).

Phase 1 lint: added time.perf_counter() + try/finally to the three _load_* helpers in RAGChain.__init__ (_load_kg, _load_generator, _load_models_sequential). Lint 13→10, drift 0/15. Change is provably latency-neutral (__init__ runs before the timed loop) — yet ALL stages regressed ~20%: query_processing 55→68ms p50, hybrid_search 89→130ms p50, reranking 1447→1864ms p50, embedding cold 1642→2489ms p95. Uniform cross-stage regression indicates GPU/machine-wide noise, not the code change. Reverted per strict keep rule. NOISE FLOOR CONCERN: ~400ms jitter (13% of baseline) swamps Phase 2 expected wins (200-400ms, 7-13%). Need to either (a) multi-run and median the result, (b) recapture baseline on hot GPU, or (c) bundle larger changes per iteration.
